meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Bridge your experience gap with marketing automation. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade portfolio projects that get you hired.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a high-impact marketing automation tool. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can deploy a functional SaaS product that demonstrates professional engineering competence, system architecture design, and the ability to solve real-world business problems efficiently.
content.intro:
The job market doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about what you've shipped. You're staring at a blank repository, the 'no portfolio' ghost haunting every application you send. It’s a paralyzing cycle: you need experience to get the job, but you need the job to get the experience. 

Today, we break that cycle. We aren't building a to-do list; we are architecting a professional-grade marketing automation engine. This is your ticket to proving you can handle complex API integrations, state management, and production-ready deployments. Let’s turn that silence into a signal.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Establish the core workflow: trigger, filter, and action. For this project, we focus on an automated email-lead enrichment pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular architecture for a marketing automation engine that handles incoming webhooks, filters leads by domain, and triggers an enrichment API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --plan 'Initialize project structure with Next.js and Tailwind, ensuring modular architecture for the automation engine.'

steps.title: Build the Data Ingestion Layer
steps.description: Create a robust webhook listener. Reliability is the difference between a student project and a production app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Express.js webhook handler with Zod validation for incoming lead data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement webhook handler with Zod schemas for payload validation'

steps.title: Integrate External APIs
steps.description: Connect your engine to a service like Clearbit or Hunter.io to show you can handle third-party authentication and rate limits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service layer for API integration that includes exponential backoff for rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate Clearbit API with robust error handling and retry logic'

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Track the status of each automation run. This demonstrates your understanding of database transactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Prisma schema for tracking automation jobs, statuses, and logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Initialize Prisma schema and run migrations for job tracking'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Push to production. A project isn't real until it's accessible via a live URL.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration and a basic uptime monitoring check.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure Vercel deployment and verify CI/CD pipeline'

pro_tips: Always include a 'logs' view in your UI; it proves you understand observability, which is a senior-level concern.
Implement rate-limiting headers in your API; it shows you care about security and cost control.
Use Zod for every external input; never trust the payload, even if it comes from a 'trusted' source.
Write a README that explains the 'Why' behind your architecture, not just the 'How' of the code.
Add a 'dry-run' mode to your automation; it signals to recruiters that you think about production safety.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, add a case study section to your portfolio site detailing the challenges you faced during integration and how you debugged them using Claude Code.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about building and start shipping the tools that will land you your first developer role. You have the guide, the tools, and the mandate to succeed. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
